reviewer: agent matrix pass (runner + e2e evidenced, revision 2)

rubric.evidence: conformance (headless no-GPU, WASM): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. e2e drive on a persistent profile: clicked Download (~155 MB q8) → loader ready → Detect produced real output (status 'Found 161 candidate boxes across 3 queries'), WASM, 1720 ms. Standalone probe also confirmed inference returns (22 boxes; two cats scored 0.58/0.53). Console clean. Screenshot: .verify/shots/owlvit-detection-dark.png.

Confirmed real zero-shot-object-detection output: boxes+labels+scores for the supplied text queries. On the cats sample, 'a cat' boxed both cats (57.7% best), 'a remote control' boxed both remotes, 'a blanket' matched the couch — 7 boxes shown at threshold 0.1. The See-inside per-query summary + raw-box table (pixel coords) match the model output with no post-processing beyond the score filter. Screenshot: .verify/shots/owlvit-detection-dark.png.

conformance: declares-model-id (Xenova/owlvit-base-patch32)=pass, declares-task (zero-shot-object-detection)=pass, declares-backend (WASM)=pass, declares-quantisation (q8)=pass, inference-off-main-thread (worker.js)=pass. e2e readout showed backend WASM. At-a-glance also honestly contrasts v1 vs OWLv2 (backbone/size/accuracy).

shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader). e2e cached-reload (second tab, same profile) reached state=ready with buttons=['Clear cached model'] only — NO Download/Load click for a returning user. First visit correctly showed the Download button (~155 MB).
rubric.notes: agent-verdicted: auto-init on cached model confirmed; never silently re-downloads the 155 MB model.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass, runs-without-webgpu-fallback (runs on the WASM path, no GPU needed)=confirmed in e2e. Download progress, ready, and 'Detecting…' states surfaced; failures route to an .err status; loader offers Retry. Offline use served from Cache Storage after first load.
rubric.notes: agent-verdicted: WASM path runs with no WebGPU.

rubric.evidence: Every control real + labelled + keyboard-operable: sample thumbs (role=button, Enter/Space), dropzone (role=button, drag+keyboard), the free-text query textarea, preset chips, Detect button, and the native score-threshold range with a live <output> that filters the cached boxes client-side (no re-inference). Verified on desktop + mobile screenshots.

Boxes, labels, scores, count chips, per-query table and raw-box table are all derived from the real pipeline output at a low floor (0.01); the slider only filters the cached list. No canned result. Changing the query text re-runs the model (new text embeddings). Confirmed in e2e (.verify/shots/owlvit-detection-dark.png).

conformance: images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Sample images carry descriptive alt; the canvas has an aria-label; status is an aria-live region; the stage canvas is focusable with a visible focus ring. Light + dark both render with the WCAG-AA design-system palette (screenshots -light.png / -dark.png).

responsive matrix: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass (no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, console+network clean at 1280×800 and 360×740 DPR3). Agent read both screenshots — headings scale, the query field + v1-vs-v2 table wrap, tap targets adequate. support flipped to ok/ok. Screens: reports/responsive/screens/owlvit-detection/{desktop,mobile}.png.

Inference runs entirely in a Web Worker (inference-off-main-thread=pass); the score slider filters the cached box list on the main thread (cheap array filter + canvas redraw), never re-running the model — so dragging it is INP-safe. The model runs once per query set at a low floor.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing (min-inline-size: 0) and logical properties instead of fixed widths so control panels and wide tables never overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the query field textarea and slider set min-inline-size:0; the stage canvas is inline-size:100% with max-block-size; the v1-vs-v2 and box tables use font-size scaling; preset chips flex-wrap.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive matrix: no horizontal overflow at 360×740 or 1280×800 (reports/responsive/screens/owlvit-detection/)

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all OWLViT inference runs off the main thread in worker.js; the slider only filters a cached array and redraws the canvas, never re-inferring.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; e2e slider interaction did not re-run the model

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); the page links the shared stylesheet.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions off the critical path; avoid long synchronous work on input.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — detection is a one-shot worker call at a low floor; the interactive threshold control is a pure client-side filter of the cached results, so repeated slider input stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: run-once-then-filter pattern (worker.js FLOOR=0.01 + main-thread filter); e2e console clean

openQuestions: OWLViT (v1) is more literal than OWLv2 on abstract phrases (wild page) — a side-by-side capture of the same query on both pages would quantify the recall gap.
q8 quantisation may cost a little detection accuracy vs the fp32 build; not measured here.

followUpGoals.goal: Capture the same abstract queries on the OWLViT wild page and the OWLv2 wild page to document the v1-vs-v2 recall difference with real evidence.

summary: Revision-2 agent matrix pass for owlvit-detection (zero-shot / open-vocabulary object detection, Xenova/owlvit-base-patch32, the ORIGINAL OWLViT — distinct checkpoint from the built OWLv2). Real in-browser inference verified end-to-end on a no-GPU runner (WASM q8, 1720 ms, boxes on both cats + both remotes + blanket), cached auto-init confirmed (no Download button on return, never silently re-downloads 155 MB), mobile+desktop responsive matrix clean and screenshot-verified, light+dark both render. All auto-checkable conformance assertions pass; manual dimensions agent-verdicted from real e2e output. The page honestly contrasts v1 vs v2 (backbone, size, accuracy) rather than overstating v1.
